items.title: ‘Friendships ended’: Jewish Australians reveal impact of antisemitism

children.content: The Royal Commission on Antisemitism and Social Cohesion will hear from people with lived experience in public sessions beginning in Sydney this week.

items.title: Busloads of tourists turn quiet street into 'internet sensation'

children.content: For years, the residents of Tasman Drive in Gerringong knew they lived in a stunningly beautiful part of the world, but then social media discovered it too.

image.altText: Aerial photo of a street with ocean views opposite a grassy headland

items.title: High school girls use makeshift stretcher to rescue injured hiker

children.content: A group of high school students use a makeshift stretcher and put their Duke of Edinburgh Award training to the test while rescuing an injured hiker.

image.altText: A man is carried on a makeshift stretcher through the bush.

items.title: Community anger over lack of warnings about whale carcass removal

children.content: A 25-tonne whale carcass was towed past more than a dozen South Coast beaches on Friday, attracting sharks in its wake, but local swimmers and surfers say they were given no warning.

gallery.altText: image of boat ramp with boat to left, dead whale in centre and a digger hoisting it up.

items.title: New 24-hour-a-day Sydney bus route 'a case study in community advocacy'

children.content: Communities fighting to have their local bus or ferry routes restored should campaign hard and "stick it out for the long term", according to these residents in Pyrmont.

image.altText: A group of people stood at a bus stop.

items.title: Sister shares memories of 24yo climber Angela Ha who died at Arapiles

children.content: Michelle Ha's older sister Angela died after falling from the Organ Pipes while rock climbing at Arapiles in western Victoria last week.

image.altText: Woman closed mouth smiling in a selfie on top of a mountain

items.title: Mercury would 'still be alive': Shark victim's family welcomes new drone plan

children.content: Mike Psillakis and his twin brother's widow, Maria, join authorities to announce a program that will see surfers become drone pilots.

gallery.altText: Mike Psillakis and Maria Psillakis hodl onto a drone at a beach in sydney

thumbnail.altText: Mike Psillakis and Maria Psillakis hodl onto a drone at a beach in sydney

items.title: Man accused of murdering parents and brother in 'grisly' attack

children.content: A father, mother and brother have died after a family member allegedly assaulted them at a Rosemeadow home in Sydney's south-west.

image.altText: Three investigator vans parked on a suburban street with neighbours watching.

items.title: Glitter, vows and Dancing Queens: Trundle's ABBA Festival is back in full swing

children.content: For one day each year, the town of 335 people swaps tractors for tribute acts, grain trucks for glitter and everyday life for an all-out ABBA extravaganza.

image.altText: A group of ladies dressed in pink costumes stand in front of an ABBA sign in the crowd

items.title: The cafe meeting, the $2m bounty and the botched hit that shocked Sydney

children.content: As the house of Hamzy began to fall one of its senior members hatched a million-dollar plan to take out rivals in revenge. What followed has been a secret for five years.

gallery.altText: CCTV of three men, two blurred, sat at a table

thumbnail.altText: CCTV of three men, two blurred, sat at a table

items.title: Marshall slams 'unacceptable' Tigers after horror second stanza

children.content: Benji Marshall said he could not recognise his Wests Tigers side after a humiliating second half saw Cronulla dominate at Shark Park, while there were also wins for Newcastle and Penrith on a bumper Sunday of NRL football.

items.title: Mongolian family bucks trends, hitting the road amid fuel crisis

children.content: While many Australians are thinking twice about big road trips due to fuel costs, the Sharavnyam family from Mongolia is determined to fulfil a dream to travel the world.

image.altText: A man and woman sit in the front seats of a camper van, while three kids lie on a bed built into the van.

items.title: 'I made the choice': Why James Valentine resolved to die on his own terms

children.content: Broadcaster James Valentine was offered two different treatments for his cancer, so he chose the one that let him keep doing what he loved. Australian Story followed Valentine over his final 12 months as he faced the end with courage, purpose and humour.

items.title: Youth offending falls in crime hotspot but adult rates more stubborn

children.content: Two years on from a NSW government crackdown on youth crime, one of the state's most affected towns is seeing results.

image.altText: An unidentified teenager stands next to wire fence and checks a phone

items.title: 'If the cops came, you'd slip out the back': The shifting role of Aussie pubs

children.content: From a tiny Riverina pub to a polished inner-city institution, Australia's local pubs are changing hands at record pace. As big money pours in, what happens to the communities that built them?

image.altText: Graphic illustration of arms and hands holding schooners of beer 'cheersing' under old wrought iron balcony decoration

items.title: Retro 1980s train returns to tracks after major refurbishment

children.content: As train travel grows in popularity amid the fuel crisis, an iconic regional service returns to the tracks after a major makeover.

items.title: 'Inhumane': Mick's disgust at cops' justification over the tasering of his 95yo mum

children.content: In an in-depth interview ahead of the inquest into his mother Clare Nowland's nursing home death, Mick says the family wants changes that will prevent another tragedy.

image.altText: An older man wearing a short-sleeve black button up shirt stands in his backyard in front of a green fence and hedges.

items.title: It just cost $2,553 to fill up 'Whiskers' — and Australians might bear the cost

children.content: On Australia's busiest freight route, it takes four shots of coffee, $2,553 of fuel and a little hair metal to keep the wheels of the economy turning.

image.altText: Sean Mulvaney wears gloves as he holds a petrol pump hose and fills up a blue and red truck that towers beyond the frame.

items.title: Will changes to key Sydney corridor be another Rozelle Interchange fiasco？

children.content: One of the biggest changes to the Warringah Freeway, which links Sydney's north to the CBD, takes effect on Saturday.

items.title: A-League finals result in mixed bag for World Cup bolters

children.content: Socceroos hopeful Marcus Younis sees his season come to an end but a host of Australian young guns progress after an enthralling pair of A-League finals.

image.altText: Two soccer players argue with each other.

items.title: Ukrainian vet saved humans and animals before finding refuge in Australia

children.content: Wildlife veterinarian Maria Mykytiuk led an idyllic life in Bucha, Ukraine, before Russian forces invaded the city in 2022.

image.altText: Ukrainian woman sits on ground surrounded by her young son and daughter. They are wrapped in a blanket covered in sunflowers.
